paragraph.heading: In general.—
paragraph.content: The Commandant shall establish a training program for Coast Guard child development center employees and satisfactory completion of the training program shall be a condition of employment for each employee of a Coast Guard child development center.

paragraph.heading: Timing for new hires.—
paragraph.content: The Commandant shall require each employee of a Coast Guard child development center to complete the training program established under paragraph (1) not later than 6 months after the date on which the employee is hired.

paragraph.heading: Minimum requirements.—
paragraph.chapeau: The training program established under paragraph (1) shall include, at a minimum, instruction with respect to—

subparagraph.content: activities and disciplinary techniques appropriate to children of different ages;
child abuse and neglect prevention and detection; and
cardiopulmonary resuscitation and other emergency medical procedures.

paragraph.heading: Use of department of defense programs.—
paragraph.content: The Commandant may use Department of Defense training programs, on a reimbursable or nonreimbursable basis, for purposes of this subsection.

paragraph.heading: Specialist required.—
paragraph.content: The Commandant shall require that at least 1 employee at each Coast Guard child development center be a specialist in training and curriculum development with appropriate credentials and experience.

paragraph.heading: Duties.—
paragraph.chapeau: The duties of the specialist described in paragraph (1) shall include—

subparagraph.content: daily oversight and instruction of other child care employees;
daily assistance in the preparation of lesson plans;
assisting with child abuse and neglect prevention and detection; and
advising the director of the center on the performance of the other child care employees.

paragraph.heading: Competitive service.—
paragraph.content: Each specialist described in paragraph (1) shall be an employee in a competitive service position.

note.heading: Amendments
note.p: 2018—Pub. L. 115–282 renumbered section 554 of this title as this section.
